heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Built for Wembley
metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Wembley homes — kitchens, offices and garden rooms, built in weeks and installed on site in days.
intro:
Wembley homes work hard. Whether you're in a 1930s semi near Wembley Park, a bay-fronted terrace in Sudbury or a family house within sight of the stadium arch, the story is often the same: a home you love, in a neighbourhood you don't want to leave, that's simply run out of room.

That's exactly where we come in. EazyBase designs and delivers modular home extensions in Wembley — kitchens, dining spaces, home offices, playrooms and garden rooms — precision-built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks, then installed at your home in under a week. Less disruption, fewer unknowns, and a finished space you can plan your life around.

Because the build happens off site, your garden isn't a construction zone for months and your household keeps functioning while we work. For busy Wembley families — often with several generations under one roof — that matters more than almost anything.

localAngles.heading: Extending a Metroland semi
localAngles.body: Much of Wembley Park and Sudbury was built between the wars, when the Metropolitan Railway turned fields into 'Metroland'. Those 1930s semis are generous by London standards — solid brick, decent gardens, good proportions — but their kitchens and rear rooms were designed for a very different way of living. A single-storey rear extension or a garden room suits these houses beautifully: it opens up the back of the home without touching the period character at the front, and a factory-built module can be designed to sit comfortably alongside the original brickwork.

localAngles.heading: Room for the whole family
localAngles.body: Wembley is one of London's great multigenerational neighbourhoods, and plenty of local households include grandparents, adult children or extended family under one roof. An extension often isn't a luxury here — it's how a home keeps working for everyone. We regularly design spaces that do double duty: a larger kitchen-diner where the whole family actually fits, a garden room that gives an older relative their own quiet space, or a home office that frees up a bedroom. Tell us how your household lives and we'll design around it.

localAngles.heading: Planning with Brent Council
localAngles.body: Most of the extensions we build in Wembley fall within permitted development rights, which often cover single-storey rear extensions and outbuildings such as garden rooms — though you should always check with Brent Council before starting, as rights vary by property and some homes near conservation areas or in flats and maisonettes are treated differently. We'll help you understand what applies to your home, prepare drawings, and handle building regulations. If a full planning application is needed, a precisely engineered factory-built design gives the planners clear, accurate information from day one.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works in Wembley
localAngles.body: Anyone who has lived through the stadium's event days knows Wembley's streets can be busy, and many local roads are tightly parked. A traditional build means months of deliveries, skips and trades coming and going. Our approach compresses all of that: your extension is built in our Blackburn factory while your home stays untouched, then installed on site in under a week. We plan delivery and access around your street — and around the events calendar where needed — so the disruptive part of the project is measured in days, not seasons.

faqs.q: How long does a modular extension take for a Wembley home?
faqs.a: Your extension is built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks while your home carries on as normal. Once it's ready, installation at your Wembley property typically takes under a week. Compared with a traditional build, you spend far less time living alongside the works — and you know your completion date before we start.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission from Brent Council?
faqs.a: Often not. Permitted development rights frequently cover single-storey rear extensions and garden rooms, though the rules depend on your property's history, size limits and location — so always check with Brent Council first. We'll assess your home at the design stage, advise on whether permitted development applies, and support you through a planning application if one is needed.

faqs.q: My street is tightly parked — can you still deliver and install?
faqs.a: Yes. Access planning is part of every EazyBase project. We survey your street and rear access before we build, plan the delivery route and any lifting equipment accordingly, and arrange suspensions or permits where required. In Wembley we also keep an eye on the stadium events calendar so installation day doesn't clash with match-day traffic.

faqs.q: What do Wembley homeowners typically use their extensions for?
faqs.a: The most common projects locally are open-plan kitchen-diners on the back of 1930s semis and terraces, garden rooms used as home offices or quiet space for relatives, and playrooms that give growing families breathing room. Every module is designed around how your household actually lives, so no two are quite the same.
